revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=片山八幡社| 所在地=[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]村中408| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|17|51|N|136|54|17|E|scale:25000}} | 祭神=[[応神天皇]]
[[少彦名命]]| 社格=式内社論社・旧郷社| 創建=貞観13年(871年)5月15日| 本殿=八幡造| 例祭=[[10月10日]]| 神事=| 画像=}} '''片山八幡社'''（かたやまはちまんしゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]にある[[八幡神|八幡神社]]（[[神社]]）である。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』の[[尾張国]][[春日部郡]]に記す「片山神社」の[[論社]]で、[[社格]]は[[郷社|旧郷社]]。 == 祭神 == *[[応神天皇]] *[[少彦名命]] == 由緒 == 由緒によれば創建は[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]13年([[871年]])[[5月15日 (旧暦)|5月15日]]。現在の[[京都府]][[八幡市]]にある[[石清水八幡宮]]の[[祭神]]を[[分霊]]し、この地に祭ったのが始まりとされている。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html 片山八幡社（小牧サービス）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かたやまはちまんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:小牧市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{画像提供依頼|片山八幡社|date=2010年7月}} {{神社| 名称=片山八幡社| 所在地=[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]村中408| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|17|51|N|136|54|17|E|scale:25000}} | 祭神=[[応神天皇]]
[[少彦名命]]| 社格=式内社論社・旧郷社| 創建=貞観13年(871年)5月15日| 本殿=八幡造| 例祭=[[10月10日]]| 神事=| 画像=}} '''片山八幡社'''（かたやまはちまんしゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[小牧市]]にある[[八幡神|八幡神社]]（[[神社]]）である。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』の[[尾張国]][[春日部郡]]に記す「片山神社」の[[論社]]で、[[社格]]は[[郷社|旧郷社]]。 == 祭神 == *[[応神天皇]] *[[少彦名命]] == 由緒 == 由緒によれば創建は[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]13年([[871年]])[[5月15日 (旧暦)|5月15日]]。現在の[[京都府]][[八幡市]]にある[[石清水八幡宮]]の[[祭神]]を[[分霊]]し、この地に祭ったのが始まりとされている。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html 片山八幡社（小牧サービス）] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かたやまはちまんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:小牧市]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name=Katayama Hachiman Shrine | Image=[[File:Katayamahachimansha 1.jpg|250px]] | Location=[[Aichi Prefecture]] [[Komaki City]] Muranaka 408 | Position={{coord|35|17|51|N|136|54|17|E|scale:25000}} | Deities=[[Emperor Ōjin]]
[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | ShrineRank=Shikinaisha Ronsha, Former Gōsha | Foundation=Jōgan 13 (871), May 15 | MainHall=Hachiman-zukuri | AnnualFestival=[[October 10]] | Rituals= }} '''Katayama Hachiman Shrine''' (片山八幡社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡神社}} ({{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}}) located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} under {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (division of spirit). == External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Komaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[ja:片山八幡社]]

It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (division of spirit). == External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Komaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]][[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:片山八幡社]]

[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | ShrineRank=Shikinaisha Ronsha, Former Gōsha | Foundation=Jōgan 13 (871), May 15 | MainHall=Hachiman-zukuri | AnnualFestival=[[October 10]] | Rituals= }} '''Katayama Hachiman Shrine''' (片山八幡社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡神社}} ({{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}}) located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} under {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (division of spirit). == External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Komaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:Mass editing to refresh the first new Wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[category{Katayama Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:片山八幡社]]

]][[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | ShrineRank=Shikinaisha Ronsha, Former Gōsha | Foundation=Jōgan 13 (871), May 15 | MainHall=Hachiman-zukuri | AnnualFestival=]][[October 10]] | Rituals=}}'''Katayama Hachiman Shrine''' (片山八幡社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡神社}} ({{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}}) located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} under {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.== Deities ==* ]][[Emperor Ōjin]]* ]][[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]== History ==According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (division of spirit).== External Links ==* [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)]{{Shinto Horizontal}}{{shinto-stub}}]][[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]]]][[Category:History of Komaki]]]][[Category:Buildings in Komaki]]]][[Category:Shikinaisha of Owari Province]]]][[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]]]][[Category:Hachiman It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} under {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (division of It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} under {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (division of It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} under {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (division of spirit). == External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Katayama Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahachimansha 1.jpg|250px]] | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]] [[Komaki City]] Muranaka 408 | Position={{coord|35|17|51|N|136|54|17|E|scale:25000}} | deity=[[Emperor Ōjin]]
[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | ShrineRank=Shikinaisha Ronsha, Former Gōsha | Foundation=Jōgan 13 (871), May 15 | MainHall=Hachiman-zukuri | AnnualFestival=[[October 10]] | Rituals= }} '''Katayama Hachiman Shrine''' (片山八幡社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡神社}} ({{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}}) located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} under {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (division of spirit). == External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} under {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (division of It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} under {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (division of spirit). == External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Katayama Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahachimansha 1.jpg|250px]] | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]] [[Komaki City]] Muranaka 408 | Position={{coord|35|17|51|N|136|54|17|E|scale:25000}} | deity=[[Emperor Ōjin]]
[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | ShrineRank=Shikinaisha Ronsha, Former Gōsha | Foundation=Jōgan 13 (871), May 15 | MainHall=Hachiman-zukuri | AnnualFestival=[[October 10]] | Rituals= }} '''Katayama Hachiman Shrine''' (片山八幡社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡神社}} ({{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}}) located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} under {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (division of spirit). == External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Katayama Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Katayamahachimansha 1.jpg|250px]] | location=[[Aichi Prefecture]] [[Komaki City]] Muranaka 408 | Position={{coord|35|17|51|N|136|54|17|E|scale:25000}} | deity=[[Emperor Ōjin]]
[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | ShrineRank=Shikinaisha Ronsha, Former Gōsha | Foundation=Jōgan 13 (871), May 15 | MainHall=Hachiman-zukuri | AnnualFestival=[[October 10]] | Rituals= }} '''Katayama Hachiman Shrine''' (片山八幡社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|ja|八幡神社}} ({{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}}) located in {{ill|Komaki, Aichi|ja|小牧市}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|ja|愛知県}}. It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} under {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (division of spirit). == External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市|12=draft}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊|12=draft}} (division of spirit). == External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Komaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:片山八幡社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hachiman Shrine}}

It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市|12=draft}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊|12=draft}} (division of spirit). == External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|片山八幡社|version=79909951|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Komaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha Shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:片山八幡社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katayama Hachiman Shrine}}

It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under {{ill|Owari Province|ja|尾張国|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata, Kyoto|ja|八幡市|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, through a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (division of spirit). == External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|片山八幡社|version=79909951|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Komaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:片山八幡社]]

[[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] | ShrineRank=Shikinaisha Ronsha, Former Gō-sha | Foundation=Jōgan 13 (871), May 15 | MainHall=Hachiman-zukuri | AnnualFestival=[[October 10]] | Rituals= }} '''Katayama Hachiman Shrine''' (片山八幡社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|qq=}} ([[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]) located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == Deities == * [[Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]] in present-day [[:en:Yawata|Yawata, Kyoto]], through a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (division of spirit). == External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|片山八幡社|version=79909951|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Komaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Komaki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Katayama Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:片山八幡社]]

It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 13 (871), on May 15. '''Katayama Hachiman Shrine''' (片山八幡社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} ([[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]) located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
== History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]] in present-day [[:en:Yawata|Yawata, Kyoto]], through a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (division of spirit).
== External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)]

'''Katayama Hachiman Shrine''' (片山八幡社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} ([[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]) located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
== History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]] in present-day [[:en:Yawata|Yawata, Kyoto]], through a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (division of spirit).
== External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)]

'''Katayama Hachiman Shrine''' (片山八幡社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} ([[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]) located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
== History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]] in present-day [[:en:Yawata|Yawata, Kyoto]], through a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (division of spirit).

'''Katayama Hachiman Shrine''' (片山八幡社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} ([[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]) located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Deities ==
* [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
* [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]

== History ==
According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]] in present-day [[:en:Yawata|Yawata, Kyoto]], through a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (division of spirit).

It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]] in present-day [[:en:Yawata|Yawata, Kyoto]], through a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (division of spirit).

It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
== History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]] in present-day [[:en:Yawata|Yawata, Kyoto]], through a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (division of spirit).
== External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)]

'''Katayama Hachiman Shrine''' (片山八幡社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} ([[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]) located in [[:en:Komaki|Komaki, Aichi]], [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under [[:en:Owari Province|Owari Province]] and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
== History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine]] in present-day [[:en:Yawata|Yawata, Kyoto]], through a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (division of spirit).
== External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)]

It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
== History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata|en|Yawata|WD=Q260923|lt=Yawata, Kyoto}}, through a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=bunrei}} (division of spirit).

== Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
== History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata|en|Yawata|WD=Q260923|lt=Yawata, Kyoto}}, through a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=bunrei}} (division of spirit).
== External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Katayama Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Katayamahachimansha 1.jpg | location={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}} [[Komaki City]] Muranaka 408 | Position={{coord|35|17|51|N|136|54|17|E|scale:25000}} | deity={{ill|Ōjin|WD=Q317997}} | ShrineRank=Shikinaisha Ronsha, Former Gō-sha | Foundation=Jōgan 13 (871), May 15 | MainHall=Hachiman-zukuri | AnnualFestival=[[October 10]] | Rituals= | prefecture={{ill|Komaki|WD=Q389537}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.2975|N|136.90472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Katayama Hachiman Shrine''' (片山八幡社) is a {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡宮}} ({{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}) located in {{ill|Komaki|en|Komaki|WD=Q389537|lt=Komaki, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|WD=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
== History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata|en|Yawata|WD=Q260923|lt=Yawata, Kyoto}}, through a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=bunrei}} (division of spirit).
== External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)]

It is a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社|1=candidate shrines|2=ja|3=論社|lt=candidate|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ronsha|13=User:Immanuelle/candidate shrines|1=Engishiki_Jinmyōchō#Ronsha|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho|lt=Ronsha}}}} (theory-associated shrine) listed as "Katayama Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|WD=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}} and is a former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Deities == * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * [[Sukunabikona-no-Mikoto]]
== History == According to the shrine's history, its foundation dates back to {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan|1=Zhenguan}} 13 (871), on May 15. It is said to have been established by enshrining the deities of {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachiman Shrine|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} in present-day {{ill|Yawata|en|Yawata|WD=Q260923|lt=Yawata, Kyoto|1=Yawata}}, through a {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=bunrei|1=Bunrei}} (division of spirit).
== External Links == * [http://www.kskn.co.jp/komakiview4.html Katayama Hachiman Shrine (Komaki Service)]
